title: Bifurcation analysis and phase portraits for chiral solitons with bohm potential in quantum hall effect
author: Tang, Yildirim, et al.

(1) of the previous section. U(őĺ) is real-valued function, whcih represents the shape of soliton. V stands for the velocity of soliton. The coefficient őĽ represents the wave number of solitons, while őľ and ŌČ represent the soliton frequency and phase constant, respectively. steps.title: Mathematical analysis and phase portraits for model (1)
steps.instruction: The derivation of the exact solutions follows a standard dynamical-systems based traveling-wave reduction. We first apply the traveling-wave ansatz (2), which converts the partial differential Eq. (1) into an ordinary differential equation for the real amplitude U(őĺ). This reduction isolates the soliton profiles in the comoving frame. The resulting second-order ODE is then rewritten as a planar dynamical system (5) with an associated Hamiltonian (6), where the parameter h represents the energy level of the reduced system. Different choices of the signs and magnitudes of (m1h,m2h) correspond to different phase-portrait topologies (centers, saddles, homoclinic orbits, heteroclinic orbits, and periodic orbits). Closed orbits yield periodic and elliptic-function solutions, homoclinic orbits yield localized bright or dark solitons, while heteroclinic orbits produce kink-type solutions. This systematic classification links each exact solution directly to a specific orbit in phase space, ensuring that all reported solutions are dynamically consistent with the bifurcation structure.

steps.title: Mathematical analysis and phase portraits for model (1)
steps.instruction: It is notable that the classification of parameter group (m1h,m2h,h) determines the dynamical behavior of system (5). For convenience, we denote that G(U)=m1hU3+m2hU.
